phenomenon: Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface
key_measurement: Silanol (Si–OH) surface density on fully hydroxylated amorphous silica: 4.6–4.9 OH/nm² (Zhuravlev model, 2000); Si–O bond length 1.62 Å; Si–O–Si bond angle ~144°
institutions: Russian Academy of Sciences Institute of Physical Chemistry (Zhuravlev group)

sources.citation: Zhuravlev, L.T. (2000) 'The surface chemistry of amorphous silica. Zhuravlev model', Colloids and Surfaces A: Physicochemical and Engineering Aspects 173(1–3):1–38

Dove, P.M. & Rimstidt, J.D. (1994) 'Silica-water interactions', in Heaney, P.J., Prewitt, C.T., Gibbs, G.V. (eds.) Silica: Physical Behavior, Geochemistry and Materials Applications, Reviews in Mineralogy 29:259–308, Mineralogical Society of America
Iler, R.K. (1979) 'The Chemistry of Silica: Solubility, Polymerization, Colloid and Surface Properties, and Biochemistry', Wiley-Interscience, New York

visualization_compliance: Silanol (Si–OH) surface density must use Zhuravlev model value (~4.6–4.9 OH/nm²) — do not depict higher or lower density without cited experimental source
Si–O bond length must be rendered at 1.62 Å ± 0.02 Å; Si–O–Si bond angle ~144° must be geometrically consistent
Distinguish isolated silanols, geminal silanols, and vicinal silanol pairs — do not conflate
Water adsorption layer at interface must reflect multilayer BET structure at ambient humidity — not a bulk-water slab
No speculative surface reconstructions — depict only structures documented by X-ray crystallography or neutron scattering
Molecular scale bar in nanometers required; label MOLECULAR MODEL — NOT TO SCALE for atom-size representations
